title: Riverfront/Orleans BRT Streetscape Improvements

description: This 12-foot sculpture consists of a colossal bronze head topped with graduated vessels referencing cultural traditions of enslaved Africans. The head will sit atop a concrete base covered with vertical steel panels in the shape of the Brooks slave ship.

plainSummary: This facilities project is currently in design with a total budget of $300K, expected to wrap up Fall 2026.

title: Broad Street Streetscape - Phase II w/BRT Expansion
description: Multimodal safety and operational improvements to the 0.5 mile stretch of Broad Street from Hamilton Street to Commonwealth Avenue. Improvements include two new Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) curbside stations, sidewalk and ADA accessible ramp improvements, pedestrian crossing improvements, access management, and other streetscape amenities.
location: Broad Street from Hamilton Street to Commonwealth Avenue
plainSummary: This roads & bridges project is currently on hold with a total budget of $26.8M.

title: Cary Street Sidewalk Improvements
description: Installation of dedicated pedestrian infrastructure where existing pedestrian infrastructure is lacking. New sidewalk and islands are proposed on the south side of the intersection to provide a safe connection from the adjacent neighborhoods to St. Catherine's School immediately north of the intersection, and to provide a safe refuge for pedestrians to cross Cary Street. A section of sidewalk is also proposed on St. Catherine's Lane to complete the safe pedestrian-specific link across the intersection.
location: Cary Street at the intersection of Three Chopt Road
plainSummary: This roads & bridges project is currently in design with a total budget of $586K, expected to wrap up Late 2028.

title: Clay Street Streetscape
description: Convert and improve the typical section of Clay Street from a two-lane, one-way street to a two-lane, two-way street along the 0.5 mile stretch between Arthur Ashe Boulevard and Belleville Street by providing a 10� travel lane in each direction, a 6� bike lane along the eastbound side of the corridor, and a parking lane on both sides of the corridor between Sheppard Street and Roseneath Road. This project will further improve multimodal safety and operations by providing traffic calming and access management through curb bump-outs and removing redundant entrances to parcels, and by providing bike, pedestrian, and transit access improvements and crossing accommodations at two intersections and at two bus stops.
location: Clay Street from Arthur Ashe Boulevard to Belleville Street
plainSummary: This roads & bridges project is currently on hold with a total budget of $18.8M.

title: Gillies Creek Greenway - Phase I
description: Installation of a 10� paved path, street crossing improvements at Main Street and at Williamsburg Avenue, roadway lighting improvements at Williamsburg Avenue, Government Road, and Jennie Scher Road, and bike, pedestrian and transit access improvements at eight transit stops/stations.
location: Main Street to Williamsburg Avenue
plainSummary: This roads & bridges project is currently in design with a total budget of $5.3M, expected to wrap up Mid 2030.

title: Carnation Street Sidewalks - Phase II
description: Construction of a new 5-foot wide sidewalk for approximately 0.2 miles along eastbound Carnation Street from Hioaks Road to Old Warwick Road.
location: Carnation Street from Hioaks Road to Old Warwick Road
plainSummary: This roads & bridges project is currently on hold with a total budget of $873K.

title: Mayo Island Redevelopment
description: Redevelopment of Mayo Island, including demolition of existing properties and stabilization of the island. This work is required per the grant agreement funding the purchase of Mayo Island in 2023.
